title: Microsoft considers spinning off Xbox and accelerating key franchises while exploring more games on PlayStation
summary: Reports indicate that Microsoft is considering a restructuring of Xbox, including a possible spin-off, to improve profitability, with plans to expand games to PlayStation and accelerate important titles.

title: Dead by Daylight announces movie, major overhaul and more violent content in its tenth anniversary
summary: The asymmetric multiplayer horror game celebrates its tenth anniversary with a film adaptation, a massive update that will transform it, and an increase in violence.

title: Sonic developers advise the industry to learn from indies and acknowledge the difficulty of re-releasing classic games
summary: Sonic executives say AAA studios should take inspiration from indies, while regretting that they cannot re-release many older titles due to technical limitations.

title: Dishonored creators defend loading screens as an essential part of the gaming experience
summary: The developers of Dishonored believe that loading screens, far from being a flaw, contributed to the character and pacing of games from previous generations.

title: Dragon's Dogma 2 removes microtransactions and deluxe edition after strong criticism
summary: Capcom removes the microtransactions that allowed time-saving in Dragon's Dogma 2 and discontinues its deluxe edition, responding to player complaints.

title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines 2 ends its development with a big patch and community-requested features
summary: The development of the long-awaited RPG Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines 2 comes to an end after eight months, releasing an update with improvements requested by fans.

title: Miyazaki responds to investors pressuring FromSoftware to make safer sequels
summary: FromSoftware president Hidetaka Miyazaki comments on the pressure from an investment fund and the controversy at Kadokawa, hinting at unannounced games.

title: A Super Mario Bros. cartridge sells for three million dollars, setting record
summary: A sealed copy of the classic NES game Super Mario Bros. reaches a historic price at auction, considered a 'holy grail' of video game collecting.

title: EA launches advertising platform to insert ads directly into video games
summary: Electronic Arts presents a system that allows companies to place advertising within the gameplay experience, sparking debate over monetization.

title: Ghost in the Shell reboot on Prime Video to premiere in three weeks
summary: The live-action series based on the Ghost in the Shell manga will arrive on the Prime Video streaming platform next month.

title: Call of Duty brings back early access to campaign with Modern Warfare 4
summary: After three years without offering it, Call of Duty announced that those who pre-order Modern Warfare 4 can play the campaign days before launch.

title: A clue suggests that a faithful remake of Zelda: Ocarina of Time could be in development
summary: A leaked description indicates that Nintendo could be working on a 1:1 remake of the classic Ocarina of Time.

title: Capcom president reveals the key to the studio's streak of successes
summary: Capcom attributes its continued success to a focus on quality, innovation, and respect for its classic franchises, says its president.

title: A game of hide-and-seek with body camouflage sells one million copies in just four days
summary: The independent title, which involves painting your body to blend in with the environment, becomes a sales phenomenon on Steam.

title: Blizzard sues another World of Warcraft private server for massive rights infringement
summary: Blizzard continues its legal offensive against private WoW servers, accusing one of them of large-scale infringement of its intellectual property.

title: Fumito Ueda expresses his disappointment with Sony over the closure of Bluepoint Games
summary: The creator of The Last Guardian criticizes Sony for closing Bluepoint Games, the studio responsible for acclaimed remakes like Demon's Souls.

title: Frontier Developments registers trademarks Planet Resort and Planet Festival, pointing to new simulators
summary: The British studio, known for Planet Coaster and Planet Zoo, has filed two new trademarks that suggest future releases in its Planet series.

title: Gaspi, famous Spanish streamer, dies in helicopter accident in Brazil
summary: Popular content creator Gaspi, a participant in La Velada del Año 5, died tragically in a helicopter accident in Brazil.

title: The game of creating your own Witcher would be free, according to reports
summary: An unannounced title in The Witcher saga would let players create their own character and world, and would be offered as a free game.

title: Enginefall shapes up to be the most innovative survival game in years, mixing Snowpiercer with Rust
summary: The new post-apocalyptic survival game set on a train receives positive reviews in its preview, highlighting its unique approach.
